stack: Q8_0 GGUF (--no-mtp, no MTP/speculative-decoding head), llama-server -ngl 999 --parallel 4 --jinja, CTX 20480
llamacpp_build: version: 1 (8f114a9) built with GNU 13.3.0 for Linux aarch64

measured.n_dataset_note: same wp_judge-filtered subset of data/reasoning_dataset/openai_val.jsonl as gate1_f16_baseline_v4.json (n=121, identical to Gate 1) -- like-for-like, same dataset/max_tokens/filtering, confirmed by direct n match.

concurrent_sequence_smoke.evidence_note: serve.log line 'n_slots = 4' at load_model + interleaved slot ids 0-3 across print_timing lines is this build's semantic equivalent of the literal '--parallel 4' echo (this llama-server build does not print the literal word 'parallel' in its log -- see SUMMARY.md deviation note). [sieve-capture] DONE n=121 ok=121 err=0 in the driver log is the 0-failed-requests evidence: all 121 requests across the 4 concurrent slots succeeded.

headline_finding.statement: Q8 is NOT lossless on this surgically-pruned 224-expert MoE. It costs 1.507pp against the f16 master (delta -0.0151), leaving only 0.493pp of the 2pp budget for Q6/Q5 in Plan 27-03. This CONTRADICTS the v3 precedent, where Q8 was confirmed lossless (ensemble rho 0.8056). The prune appears to have made this checkpoint materially more quantization-sensitive than the unpruned v3 was.
headline_finding.visible_only_because_of_a1: This finding is visible ONLY because Gate 1 was anchored to f16 (assumption A1), not Q8. A Q8-anchored Gate 1 would have produced delta=0.000 by construction and reported 'Q8 lossless' -- a false conclusion defining away the exact question this plan exists to answer. That is the concrete payoff of the A1 override.
headline_finding.ci_overlap_caveat: The two CIs (f16 [0.7186, 0.8582], Q8 [0.6984, 0.8492]) overlap substantially given n=121 -- this does NOT establish the point-estimate drop is statistically insignificant, only that the sampling noise at this n cannot rule out a smaller or larger true gap. The 1.507pp point-estimate drop is the honest headline; it is not rounded to zero or called 'within noise'.
headline_finding.forward_flag_for_plan_03: Q8 has already consumed 1.507pp of the 2pp budget. Q6_K and Q5_K_M are now UNLIKELY to clear the f16 floor's +/-2pp band -- the ladder may stop at Q8, making Q8 the ship tier. Plan 27-03 must still MEASURE Q6/Q5 rather than assume this; a Q6 failure should not be treated as an anomaly.

v3_shipped_comparison.note: FIRST apples-to-apples v4-pruned-vs-v3 comparison -- all three are Q8 GGUF/llama.cpp. But v3's 0.8056 is a 3-SEED ENSEMBLE and this rung is SINGLE-SEED s1; the configurations differ and the gap is not a clean like-for-like. Recorded with that caveat, not as a headline. The headline is delta_vs_gate1 (Q8 vs the SAME checkpoint's own f16 master), not this cross-checkpoint comparison.

revised_interpretation.status: headline_finding above is SUPERSEDED. The measured judge_rho (0.7851092593129675) and every other field in this file are UNCHANGED and remain the historical record of what was measured -- only the INTERPRETATION is corrected here, in Plan 27-03, after the Q6 rung came back.
revised_interpretation.what_falsified_it: ladder_q6.json's Q6_K rung measured judge_rho 0.8063205167262201 -- ABOVE the f16 floor (0.8002) and ABOVE this Q8 rung (0.7851). Q6_K is a lossier compression of the SAME f16 master than Q8_0; it cannot legitimately carry more judge signal than its own source. A real quantization-sensitivity mechanism predicts monotonic decay (f16 > Q8 > Q6); the measured ordering is Q6 > f16 > Q8, which no degradation mechanism can produce.
revised_interpretation.corrected_conclusion: The -1.507pp Q8 point estimate is NOT evidence that Q8 is lossy on this checkpoint, and NOT evidence the prune increased quantization sensitivity. At n=121 single-seed, rung-to-rung deltas of 1-2pp are well inside sampling noise (CI half-widths ~7-8pp; the three rungs span 2.12pp against each other). f16, Q8, and Q6 are statistically indistinguishable on this measurement. See output/pkg-v4/ladder_q6.json's noise_floor_finding block for the full argument.
revised_interpretation.correction_source: 27-03-SUMMARY.md and output/pkg-v4/ladder_q6.json (Plan 27-03, Task 2). Note that .planning/phases/27-packaging-publication-refresh/27-02-SUMMARY.md's headline ("Q8 is NOT lossless" / "contradicts the v3 precedent") is now known-superseded prose from before the Q6 measurement existed; it is left as-written in that file (historical record) but a reader following it forward must land here.
revised_interpretation.not_touched: gate1_baseline_ref, measured, gate, concurrent_sequence_smoke, and the raw headline_finding text above are all UNCHANGED -- this block corrects the READING of those numbers, not the numbers themselves.
